Transient and frequency response of sing.title: Transient and frequency response of single stage common emitter amplifier
Transient and frequency response of sing.aim: To verify Transient and frequency response of single stage common emitter amplifier.
Transient and frequency response of sing.theory: A single-stage CE amplifier using NPN transistor has the input applied to the BE circuit & the output taken from the collector-emitter circuit,with coupling capacitor C1&C2 providing DC isoation at input & output. It offers high current gain, high current, voltage, Power gain with moderate input and output impedence. The frequency response shows that gain varies from frequency & represented by graph of magnitude versus frequency, usually plotted logrithmic scale..
procedure: Connect the circuit as per the diagram
calculate theoritacl value for gain current
Record practical value of the circuit
Transient and frequency response of sing.result: Transient and frequency response of single stage common emitter amplifier was observed and verified

Inverting and Non-inverting amplifier.title: Inverting and Non-inverting amplifier
Inverting and Non-inverting amplifier.aim: To study and design the operational amplifier configuration of Inverting and Non-inverting amplifier.
Inverting and Non-inverting amplifier.theory: An op-amp is a high gain differential amplifier with high input impedance and low output impedance. It has 2 inputs and output the non inverting input gives an output in phase with the input signal while the inverting input gives an constant gain configuration are Inverting and Non-inverting amplifier.
procedure: Connect the circuit as per the diagram
Set the required frequency in the function generator
Connect the CRO probe to the output of the amplifier
Inverting and Non-inverting amplifier.result: The op‑amplifier non‑inverting amplifier configuration is designed & investigating verified

Design of Adder & Subtractor using Op-Am.title: Design of Adder & Subtractor using Op-Amp
Design of Adder & Subtractor using Op-Am.aim: To study & design the operational amplifier configuration of adder & subtractor
Design of Adder & Subtractor using Op-Am.theory: An adder circuit can be implemented using either the non-inverting mode or differential amplifier configuration; here the inverting mode is used. The input signals are applied through resistors to the inverting terminal, while the non-inverting terminal is grounded, creating a virtual ground. A subtractor circuit scales the input signals using resistors and when this is done, it is called scaling amplifier. The output voltage Vo is equal to the voltage applied to non-inverting terminal minus voltage applied to inverting terminal.
procedure: Give the connections as per the circuit diagram
Observe the O/P & Note the readings
Compare with the theoretical values obtained from formula
Design of Adder & Subtractor using Op-Am.result: Hence adder & subtractor are designed using op-amp IC741

Transient and Frequency Response of JFET.title: Transient and Frequency Response of JFET
Transient and Frequency Response of JFET.aim: To verify transient & frequency response of JFET
Transient and Frequency Response of JFET.theory: A FET is a voltage operated device that can be used in amplifier & switching circuit. A FET requires virtually no I/P current is circuit Hence it has an extremely high i/p resistance. An N-channel JFET has an N-type channel with P-type gate.
procedure: Connect the circuit as per the circuit diagram
Set the input voltage using the function generator
For every value of frequency, note down the corresponding amplitude values
Transient and Frequency Response of JFET.result: Thus the transient & frequency response of JFET is verified. Bandwidth ≈ 300 kHz

Comparator using Op-Amp.title: Comparator using Op-Amp
Comparator using Op-Amp.aim: To design & construct a comparator using op-amp
Comparator using Op-Amp.theory: It is a circuit which compares a signal voltage applied at I/P of an op-amp with output +ve(sat) and -ve(sat). If the signal is applied to inverting terminal of op-amp, it's called inverting comparator & if signal is applied to non-inverting, it is non inverting comparator. If the I/P is less than reference voltage, O/P will be at +ve & when I/P signal is greater than the reference voltage output will be -ve. The vice versa happens in non-inverting comparator.
procedure: Connect the circuit as per the diagram
Connect an AC voltage to the non-inverting input of the op-amp
Connect reference voltage source to inverting input of the op-amp
Comparator using Op-Amp.result: Thus a comparator is designed using an op-amp.

Colpitt Oscillator.title: Colpitt Oscillator
Colpitt Oscillator.aim: To study & design a colpitt oscillator with C1=50nF, C2=50nF & L=70mH
Colpitt Oscillator.theory: The colpitt's oscillator uses 2-center tapped capacitor in series with a parallel inductor to form its resource tank circuit producing sinusoidal oscillator. The tuned tank circuit connected b/w the collector & base of the single stage transistor amplifier producing a sinusoidal output waveform. Frequency stability of oscillator is improved along with simple design.
procedure: Give the connections as per the circuit diagram
Produce the sine wave output using the circuit
Measure the amplitude & time period of the wave
Colpitt Oscillator.result: Thus the colpitt oscillator was connected & verified.

High Pass Filter.title: Design of High Pass Filter
High Pass Filter.aim: To design a first-order high-pass filter and determine its frequency range
High Pass Filter.theory: The circuit uses an op-amp for amplification and gain control, behaving as a band-pass filter with a high-frequency cutoff determined by gain selection.
procedure: Connections made as per circuit diagram
Output waveform noted by varying frequency
High Pass Filter.result: First-order high-pass filter designed with a bandwidth of approximately 240 Hz.

Low Pass Filter.title: Design of Low Pass Filter
Low Pass Filter.aim: To design a first-order low-pass filter and determine its frequency range
Low Pass Filter.theory: Basic operation similar to passive RC low-pass filter, using a single reactive component.
procedure: Output waveform noted and graphed by varying frequency
Low Pass Filter.result: First-order low-pass filter designed with a bandwidth of about 200 Hz.

Astable Multivibrator.title: Astable Multivibrator
Astable Multivibrator.aim: To design and construct an astable multivibrator using IC555 timer
Astable Multivibrator.theory: IC555 timer operates in astable mode, comparators monitor voltage, producing a square wave output.
procedure: Square wave output obtained and charging/discharging waveform observed
Astable Multivibrator.result: Astable multivibrator designed using IC555 timer and verified.

Monostable Multivibrator.title: Monostable Multivibrator
Monostable Multivibrator.aim: To design the monostable multivibrator using IC555 timer
Monostable Multivibrator.theory: A monostable multivibrator has one stable and one quasistable state. When triggered, it switches from stable to quasistable state and returns back to stable state. Duration in quasistable state determined by RC constant.
procedure: Output of pulse generator set to desired frequency
Output waveform obtained by connecting Pin 3
Monostable Multivibrator.result: Thus monostable multivibrator is designed using IC555.

Inverting Amplifier using LT-spice.theory: An inverting amplifier is an op-amp circuit where output is inverted (180° phase shift) relative to input.
procedure: Save and run to view output
Inverting Amplifier using LT-spice.result: Thus inverting amplifier is designed and verified using LT-spice.

Non-Inverting Amplifier.theory: Input applied to non-inverting terminal, output phase same as input, positive voltage gain.
